title: Power Distributors and Dispatchers
description: Coordinate, regulate, or distribute electricity or steam.

tasks.text: Coordinate with engineers, planners, field personnel, or other utility workers to provide information such as clearances, switching orders, or distribution process changes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Respond to emergencies, such as transformer or transmission line failures, and route current around affected areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Control, monitor, or operate equipment that regulates or distributes electricity or steam, using data obtained from instruments or computers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Direct personnel engaged in controlling or operating distribution equipment or machinery, such as instructing control room operators to start boilers or generators.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Distribute or regulate the flow of power between entities, such as generating stations, substations, distribution lines, or users, keeping track of the status of circuits or connections.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Manipulate controls to adjust or activate power distribution equipment or machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare switching orders that will isolate work areas without causing power outages, referring to drawings of power systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor and record switchboard or control board readings to ensure that electrical or steam distribution equipment is operating properly.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Implement energy schedules, including real-time transmission reservations or schedules.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calculate load estimates or equipment requirements to determine required control settings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Track conditions that could affect power needs, such as changes in the weather, and adjust equipment to meet any anticipated changes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record and compile operational data, such as chart or meter readings, power demands, or usage and operating times, using transmission system maps.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Inspect equipment to ensure that specifications are met or to detect any defects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Tend auxiliary equipment used in the power distribution process.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
